channel.title: Context Engineering Daily - Research Papers
channel.description: Latest Research Papers news in Context Engineering

item.title: QueryPlot: Generating Geological Evidence Layers using Natural Language Queries for Mineral Exploration
item.description: arXiv:2602.17784v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Mineral prospectivity mapping requires synthesizing heterogeneous geological knowledge, including textual deposit models and geospatial datasets, to identify regions likely to host specific mineral deposit types. This process is traditionally manual a...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> research, retrieval, framework, embedding, analysis | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Neural Synchrony Between Socially Interacting Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2602.17815v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Neuroscience has uncovered a fundamental mechanism of our social nature: human brain activity becomes synchronized with others in many social contexts involving interaction. Traditionally, social minds have been regarded as an exclusive property of li...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> large language model, context, alignment, experiment, LLM | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Improving Neural Topic Modeling with Semantically-Grounded Soft Label Distributions
item.description: arXiv:2602.17907v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Traditional neural topic models are typically optimized by reconstructing the document's Bag-of-Words (BoW) representations, overlooking contextual information and struggling with data sparsity. In this work, we propose a novel approach to construct s...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, retrieval, prompt, experiment, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Alignment in Time: Peak-Aware Orchestration for Long-Horizon Agentic Systems
item.description: arXiv:2602.17910v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Traditional AI alignment primarily focuses on individual model outputs; however, autonomous agents in long-horizon workflows require sustained reliability across entire interaction trajectories. We introduce APEMO (Affect-aware Peak-End Modulation for...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> alignment, model, LLM, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Cross-Embodiment Offline Reinforcement Learning for Heterogeneous Robot Datasets
item.description: arXiv:2602.18025v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Scalable robot policy pre-training has been hindered by the high cost of collecting high-quality demonstrations for each platform. In this study, we address this issue by uniting offline reinforcement learning (offline RL) with cross-embodiment learni...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> experiment, analysis, platform, RAG, demonstration | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Neurosymbolic Language Reasoning as Satisfiability Modulo Theory
item.description: arXiv:2602.18095v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Natural language understanding requires interleaving textual and logical reasoning, yet large language models often fail to perform such reasoning reliably. Existing neurosymbolic systems combine LLMs with solvers but remain limited to fully formaliza...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> large language model, reasoning, experiment, instruction, RAG | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: LATMiX: Learnable Affine Transformations for Microscaling Quantization of LLMs
item.description: arXiv:2602.17681v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Post-training quantization (PTQ) is a widely used approach for reducing the memory and compute costs of large language models (LLMs). Recent studies have shown that applying invertible transformations to activations can significantly improve quantizat...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> large language model, zero-shot, experiment, analysis, RAG | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Probabilistic NDVI Forecasting from Sparse Satellite Time Series and Weather Covariates
item.description: arXiv:2602.17683v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Accurate short-term forecasting of vegetation dynamics is a key enabler for data-driven decision support in precision agriculture. Normalized Difference Vegetation Index (NDVI) forecasting from satellite observations, however, remains challenging due ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> framework, transformer, experiment, RAG, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: WorkflowPerturb: Calibrated Stress Tests for Evaluating Multi-Agent Workflow Metrics
item.description: arXiv:2602.17990v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: LLM-based systems increasingly generate structured workflows for complex tasks. In practice, automatic evaluation of these workflows is difficult, because metric scores are often not calibrated, and score changes do not directly communicate the severi...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> study, release, LLM, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Optimal Multi-Debris Mission Planning in LEO: A Deep Reinforcement Learning Approach with Co-Elliptic Transfers and Refueling
item.description: arXiv:2602.17685v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This paper addresses the challenge of multi target active debris removal (ADR) in Low Earth Orbit (LEO) by introducing a unified coelliptic maneuver framework that combines Hohmann transfers, safety ellipse proximity operations, and explicit refueling...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> framework, experiment, paper, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Towards More Standardized AI Evaluation: From Models to Agents
item.description: arXiv:2602.18029v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Evaluation is no longer a final checkpoint in the machine learning lifecycle. As AI systems evolve from static models to compound, tool-using agents, evaluation becomes a core control function. The question is no longer "How good is the model?" but "C...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> tool, model, paper, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)
